events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a trench protection assessment where the field photo is paired with a cropped sheet excerpt, but the title block, revision, and matching callout are not visible. This means the installed condition cannot be tied to the controlling document. The reference material is OSHA 1926 Subpart P.

Key issue: The installed condition cannot be verified against the controlling document because the title block, revision, and matching callout are not visible. This is a documentation/traceability problem. Without being able to tie the installed condition to the controlling page, the inspector cannot confirm compliance.

Since we cannot verify the work against the controlling document, the appropriate decision is "needs_more_info" — we need the full drawing with title block, revision, and callout to confirm the trench protection matches the engineered design.

However, let me reconsider. The task says "If the work is dangerous or non-compliant, decision must be fail." The scenario doesn't describe any specific dangerous condition — it describes a documentation gap. The work has been tested/inspected but the controlling document can't be tied to the installed condition.

This is a "needs_more_info" situation because we literally cannot verify compliance without the controlling document being identifiable.

Let me construct the JSON.

    "OSHA 1926 Subpart P requires protective systems for trenches 5 ft or deeper; adequacy of the installed system against the engineered design is unverified"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Obtain the full-size drawing sheet showing title block, revision number, and the callout matching the installed trench protection",
    "Verify the installed protective system (sloping, shoring, or shielding) matches the engineered design and OSHA Subpart P requirements for the observed trench depth and soil type",
    "Confirm competent person inspection records for the trench and protective system before allowing continued entry"
  ],
  "rationale": "The field condition has been inspected but cannot be verified against the controlling document because the drawing excerpt lacks identifying information. Trench protection is life-safety critical; without confirmed traceability to the engineered design, compliance cannot be determined, so additional documentation is required before acceptance.",

    "OSHA 1926 Subpart P - 1926.652 - Requirements for protective systems",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart P - 1926.651 - Specific excavation requirements",
